short_description_of_the_course.label: Short Description of the Course
short_description_of_the_course.value: This course covers secure software engineering methods and techniques. It explores all aspects of the software engineering lifecycle that are directly applicable to designing systems where security and/or privacy is critical.

requirements_of_entry.label: Requirements of Entry
value: Object Oriented Software Engineering (COMPSCI2008) or its equivalent. Visiting students would need a firm background in object oriented programming such as Java.

timetable_and_length_and_frequency_of_te.label: Timetable and length and frequency of teaching sessions
timetable_and_length_and_frequency_of_te.value: 2 hours of lecture time and 1 hour of tutorial or practical work, per week

course_aims.label: Course Aims
course_aims.value:
Broadly, this course aims to offer practical knowledge on engineering secure systems across the software engineering life-cycle. These include requirements, design, implementation, testing and maintenance. Topics covered include secure design principles and patterns, eliciting security requirements and uncovering security flaws in software design. This is in addition to fundamental privacy engineering concepts such as Privacy-by-Design and other the conceptual framework for understanding privacy in software. This course also devotes a significant time to discussing well known secure design solutions including architectural patterns and design patterns focusing on security/privacy countermeasures. We discuss information-flow analysis and evaluation as mechanisms to assess the effectiveness of the secure design solutions implemented in source code.

The course will help prepare students to apply software security and privacy techniques in industry, as well as to pursue research in software engineering, security and privacy.

intended_learning_outcomes_of_course.label: Intended Learning Outcomes of Course
intended_learning_outcomes_of_course.value:
By the end of this course students will be able to:
Describe the life cycle for developing secure software systems.
Apply lightweight refactoring methods to balance trade-offs between competing security, privacy and functionality quality measures in software.
Verify the effectiveness of a secure software design solution.
Explore general approaches to privacy engineering and Privacy-by-Design paradigm in software.
Build a simple privacy justificatory framework for justifying the extent a given software aligns with data protection regulations (e.g GDPR, HIPPA, etc.).
Apply secure software design principles to a range of application domains and case studies such as social networks, internet of things, mobile computing, ecommerce, service oriented architectures, multi-agent and autonomous systems.

description_of_summative_assessment.label: Description of Summative Assessment
description_of_summative_assessment.value: 80% for the end of year exam, 20% for assessed coursework

formative_assessment_feedback.label: Formative Assessment & Feedback
formative_assessment_feedback.value: Individual written and verbal feedback will be provided on assessed exercises.Generic feedback will be provided to the class, on the assessed exercises, during lecture time. Generic feedback will be provided to the class, via Moodle, on the final exam.

additional_relevant_information.label: Additional Relevant Information
additional_relevant_information.value:
RESIT EXAMINATIONS ARE ALLOWED FOR MASTERS STUDENTS
RESIT EXAMINATIONS ARE NOT ALLOWED FOR HONOURS STUDENTS
